name: Corporate Vietnam

color_palette.description: Derived from major Vietnamese corporate brands (Vietcombank, Vingroup, VietinBank). Vietnamese corporate identity converges on national colors with professional refinement.
color_palette.note: Vietnamese corporate palettes consistently reference the national flag (red + gold) or nature (green for banking/prosperity). Blue appears in banking/tech. For the national unity topic, red + gold is most appropriate.

typography.size_scale: Corporate presentations typically use: Company name/title 48-64px, Section headers 36-44px, Body text 24-32px, Fine print 18-20px. At 1920x1080 for projector: headers 48-56px, body 28-32px minimum.
typography.weights: SemiBold (600) for headers, Regular (400) for body, Bold (700) for emphasis. Vietnamese corporate style avoids Light/Thin weights.
typography.vietnamese_diacritics: Be Vietnam Pro handles Vietnamese diacritics natively. Montserrat has acceptable but not purpose-built Vietnamese support. Corporate Vietnamese design prioritizes diacritic clarity.

animation_style.approach: Polished and restrained. Vietnamese corporate videos favor smooth slide-in transitions, gentle fade-ins, and subtle scaling effects. No playful or bouncy animations.
animation_style.entrance_patterns: Elements slide in from edges with deceleration easing. Text fades in with slight upward movement. Charts build progressively.
animation_style.motion_budget: Moderate - every section has entrance animation but effects are conservative and uniform. Consistent timing across all elements.
animation_style.note: Inspired by Vietnamese bank and real estate promotional videos - professional, confident, not flashy

projector_readability.contrast_ratio: Good. Dark text (#1A1A2E) on light surface (#F5F5F0) achieves ~14:1. Gold accent on white needs careful sizing (may drop below AA for small text).
projector_readability.minimum_font_sizes: Corporate style naturally uses professional-sized text. Body text at 28px+ is standard.
projector_readability.color_washout_resistance: Light backgrounds work well on projectors. Red and green primaries maintain visibility even with washout. Gold accents may lose vibrancy.

wcag_contrast_score.red_on_white: 7.8:1 (#C8102E on #FFFFFF) - AAA pass for large text, AA for normal
wcag_contrast_score.gold_on_white: 3.2:1 (#D4A843 on #FFFFFF) - fails AA for normal text, use only for large decorative elements
wcag_contrast_score.degradation_note: Red and green maintain AA even with 25% projector degradation. Gold needs dark background or large sizing.

projector_degradation_resistance.strengths: Light backgrounds render accurately. Bold primary colors (red, green) resist washout. Corporate layouts use generous spacing that aids readability at distance.
projector_degradation_resistance.weaknesses: Gold accents lose saturation. Subtle gradient effects (common in corporate design) become flat. Thin decorative lines disappear.
projector_degradation_resistance.recommendation: Use solid colors, avoid gradients. Keep gold for large decorative elements only. Ensure all text uses high-contrast combinations.

vietnamese_diacritics_rendering.quality: Excellent when using Be Vietnam Pro. Vietnamese corporate design has matured to prioritize diacritics rendering.
vietnamese_diacritics_rendering.line_height_needs: Corporate Vietnamese typography typically uses 1.4-1.6x line-height to accommodate diacritics comfortably
vietnamese_diacritics_rendering.minimum_safe_size: 28px with Be Vietnam Pro. Corporate Vietnamese content rarely goes below 24px even in footnotes.

cultural_fit.analysis: Vietnamese corporate identity directly references national symbols and colors. Red + gold from the national flag is a natural fit for a topic on national unity. The professional polish signals respect for the academic institution. The 'corporate Vietnam' aesthetic communicates modern Vietnamese identity - exactly the balance of tradition and progress that the national unity topic requires.
cultural_fit.adaptation_needed: Minimal - the style inherently aligns with Vietnamese cultural context. May need to soften corporate sharpness slightly for academic warmth.

cultural_semiotics.inherent_symbols: Red = revolution, national flag, Communist Party. Gold = prosperity, star on flag, Vietnamese cultural heritage. Green = growth, nature, banking stability. These are deeply embedded in Vietnamese corporate visual culture.
cultural_semiotics.strength: Colors carry patriotic meaning automatically. Teacher will recognize these as intentional, culturally aware choices.
cultural_semiotics.lotus_opportunity: Lotus motif (used by Vietnam Airlines, many Vietnamese brands) can be incorporated as decorative element for purity/national flower symbolism

teacher_perception_signal.signal: Strong positive signal. Communicates: 'We take this seriously, we understand Vietnamese professional standards, we care about quality presentation.' Corporate polish suggests student effort and organizational competence. The style mirrors what the teacher sees in professional Vietnamese media.
teacher_perception_signal.risk: Could appear overly corporate/commercial for an academic assignment if not balanced with academic content elements (citations, theoretical frameworks)

rubric_alignment.format_15_percent: Excellent - polished corporate format immediately scores well on professionalism, layout consistency, and visual quality
rubric_alignment.theory_35_percent: Good - corporate layouts support structured content: card-based theory presentations, clean section divisions, professional citations
rubric_alignment.application_50_percent: Excellent - corporate Vietnamese style naturally incorporates data visualization, case studies, statistics, and real-world examples. Charts and infographics are native to this aesthetic.

content_density_capacity.words_per_frame: High. Corporate presentations are built for information density: 80-120 words per frame with card layouts, sidebar panels, and structured lists.
content_density_capacity.theory_support: Strong - corporate reports regularly present dense analytical content with clear hierarchy
content_density_capacity.data_viz_support: Excellent - bar charts, line graphs, comparison tables, and infographics are standard corporate presentation elements

production_effort.migration_from_current: Low to moderate. Current codebase already uses red (#B91C1C) + gold (#D97706) accents on light background (#F7F3EE), which closely matches Corporate Vietnam aesthetics. Main changes: adjust surface colors, refine typography scale, add corporate layout patterns.
production_effort.estimated_effort: 1-2 days. Color palette requires minor adjustment. Layout patterns can reuse existing DS components with styling updates.
production_effort.reuse_potential: High - existing card-based layout, section titles, and citation footers align well with corporate presentation structure

remotion_implementation_complexity.css_needs: Low - clean CSS with flexbox layouts, card borders, subtle shadows. No complex CSS features.
remotion_implementation_complexity.three_js: Optional - current 3D background can be retained at low opacity or replaced with simpler gradient/solid background
remotion_implementation_complexity.render_performance: Low overhead - no heavy rendering requirements

distraction_level: Low. Corporate style prioritizes content clarity over visual flair. Animations are functional, not decorative.
differentiation: Moderate. Stands out from typical student PowerPoints by looking professionally produced, but familiar enough to not feel alien. Other Vietnamese students may use similar corporate aesthetics, reducing uniqueness.

temporal_coherence.analysis: Corporate brand guidelines are designed for consistency across long-form content. Color roles, typography hierarchy, and layout templates ensure visual unity across all 13.5 minutes. Different speakers' sections feel like chapters of the same professional report.

dark_vs_light_mode_viability: Light mode is the primary mode for Vietnamese corporate design (matching projector requirements). Dark mode is possible but less aligned with the corporate Vietnam aesthetic, which favors clean white/light surfaces.

examples: Vietcombank annual reports and promotional videos
Vingroup corporate presentations and VinFuture Prize ceremony materials
VietinBank and BIDV corporate identity materials
precedent.academic_context: Vietnamese university guest lectures from corporate speakers frequently use this style. Teachers are familiar with and generally approve of this aesthetic.
